title: Lower of Cost or Market (NRV)
event: At year-end, Reyes Co.'s inventory has a cost of $5,000 but a net realizable value of $4,600. Prepare the required entry.

entries.label: Write down to the lower figure

lines.label: 5,000 − 4,600
lines.why: The $400 decline in value is recognized NOW, in the period the value dropped.
lines.trap: Conservatism runs one way: write DOWN to market, never UP above cost.

lines.label: carried at NRV
lines.why: Inventory now carries at $4,600 — the lower of the two.
lines.trap: Credit only the DECLINE, not the whole inventory balance.

memorize.body: Compare cost to NRV and report the LOWER. Losses are recognized when value drops; gains wait until the goods actually sell.
If NRV had been $5,300, the entry is NOTHING — inventory never gets written up above cost.

distractors.feedback: That's the new carrying value. The ENTRY records only the decline: 5,000 − 4,600 = 400.
You can't wait for the sale — the loss is recognized in the period the value fell.

build.scaffold: One small entry: debit COGS (or a loss account), credit Inventory, for the DIFFERENCE only.
